FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Read these files and report key patterns: 1. `src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py` - Full file, report: PR-centric display pattern, run-name format parsing, WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP usage, constants, deduplication pattern 2. `src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py` - Full file, report: extract_pr_number() function, regex pattern used 3. Report line numbers for each pattern.
session.assistant: I'll help you analyze those files. Let me start by checking the documentation index, then read the specific files you're asking about.

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12→- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13→- [changelog/](changelog/) 14→- [checklists/](checklists/) 15→- [ci/](ci/) 16→- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17→- [cli/](cli/) — Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18→- [commands/](commands/) — Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19→- [config/](config/) 20→- [configuration/](configuration/) 21→- [documentation/](documentation/) — Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22→- [erk/](erk/) — Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23→- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24→- [gateway/](gateway/) 25→- [hooks/](hooks/) — Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26→- [integrations/](integrations/) 27→- [objectives/](objectives/) 28→- [planning/](planning/) — Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. Add docs here for planning workflow patterns. 29→- [pr-operations/](pr-operations/) 30→- [refactoring/](refactoring/)

1→"""List workflow runs command.""" 2→ 3→from datetime import UTC, datetime 4→ 5→import click 6→from rich.console import Console 7→from rich.markup import escape as escape_markup 8→from rich.table import Table 9→ 10→from erk.cli.commands.pr.list_cmd import format_pr_cell 11→from erk.cli.commands.run.shared import extract_plan_number, extract_pr_number 12→from erk.cli.constants import WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP 13→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 14→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 15→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 16→from erk.core.display_utils import ( 17→ format_submission_time, 18→ format_workflow_outcome, 19→ format_workflow_run_id, 20→) 21→from erk.core.pr_utils import select_display_pr 22→from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import format_checks_cell 23→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import github_repo_location_from_url 24→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import GitHubRepoId, PullRequestInfo, WorkflowRun 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→ 27→_MAX_DISPLAY_RUNS = 50 28→_PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT = 20 29→_MAX_TITLE_LENGTH = 50 30→
... [167 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Shared utilities for run commands.""" 2→ 3→import re 4→ 5→ 6→def extract_pr_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: 7→ """Extract PR number from display_title containing '#NNN'. 8→ 9→ Handles: 10→ - "pr-address:#456:abc123" → 456 11→ - "8559:#460:abc123" → 460 12→ - "one-shot:#458:abc123" → 458 13→ - "rebase:#456:abc123" → 456 14→ - "8559:abc123" (old format, no #) → None 15→ - "Some title [abc123]" (legacy) → None 16→ - None or empty → None 17→ """ 18→ if not display_title: 19→ return None 20→ match = re.search(r"#(\d+)", display_title) 21→ if match is None: 22→ return None 23→ return int(match.group(1)) 24→ 25→ 26→def extract_plan_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None: 27→ """Extract plan number from display_title format '123:abc456'. 28→ 29→ Handles: 30→ - New format: "123:abc456" → 123
... [17 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant:
Perfect! I've read both files. Let me provide you with a comprehensive report of the key patterns with line numbers:
## Report: Pattern Analysis
### 1. `src/erk/cli/commands/run/list_cmd.py` - Key Patterns
**PR-Centric Display Pattern (Lines 32-184)** - Entire function `_list_runs()` implements a PR-centric table view - Core design: Runs are grouped and correlated with PRs for unified display - The table layout (lines 114-121) structures columns: run-id, status, submitted, workflow, pr, title, chks
**Run-Name Format Parsing (Lines 68-79)** - Extracts PR numbers from `display_title` using `extract_pr_number()` (line 73) - Falls back to extracting plan numbers using `extract_plan_number()` (line 77) - Stores results in `run_pr_numbers` dict (line 69) for efficient lookup
**WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP Usage (Lines 42-45)** - Iterates over `WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP.items()` to get command names and workflow files - Maps each workflow file to its registered command name - Fetches runs per workflow, limited by `_PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT` (20 runs per workflow)
**Constants (Lines 27-29)** - `_MAX_DISPLAY_RUNS = 50` - maximum runs shown in output - `_PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT = 20` - runs fetched per workflow - `_MAX_TITLE_LENGTH = 50` - title truncation threshold (lines 153-154)
**Deduplication Pattern (Lines 47-52)** - Uses dict keying by `run.run_id` to deduplicate - `seen` dict tracks unique runs across all workflows - Final `tagged_runs` list contains deduplicated results
---
### 2. `src/erk/cli/commands/run/shared.py` - Key Patterns
**extract_pr_number() Function (Lines 6-23)** - Signature: `def extract_pr_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None` - Input validation (lines 18-19): Returns None if `display_title` is None or empty - **Regex Pattern Used (Line 20)**: `r"#(\d+)"` - Matches a literal '#' followed by one or more digits - Captures the digit group for extraction - Extraction (lines 21-23): Returns the captured number as int, or None if no match
**Handled Formats (Lines 9-15 docstring):** - `"pr-address:#456:abc123"` → 456 - `"8559:#460:abc123"` → 460 - `"one-shot:#458:abc123"` → 458 - `"rebase:#456:abc123"` → 456 - `"8559:abc123"` (old format, no #) → None - `"Some title [abc123]"` (legacy) → None
**extract_plan_number() Function (Lines 26-41)** - Signature: `def extract_plan_number(display_title: str | None) -> int | None` - Format: Parses `"NUMBER:HASH"` format (colon-separated) - Validation (lines 34-40): Checks for colon presence, splits, validates first part is numeric with `.isdigit()` - Returns integer plan number or None
---
### Summary Table
| Pattern | File | Lines | Details | |---------|------|-------|---------| | PR-centric display | list_cmd.py | 32-184 | Entire function builds run→PR table view | | WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP iteration | list_cmd.py | 42-45 | Fetches runs from each registered workflow | | Deduplication by run_id | list_cmd.py | 47-52 | Dict keying on `run.run_id` to eliminate duplicates | | Constants | list_cmd.py | 27-29 | MAX_DISPLAY_RUNS, PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT, MAX_TITLE_LENGTH | | extract_pr_number() | shared.py | 6-23 | Regex `#(\d+)` extracts PR number from display_title | | Regex pattern | shared.py | 20 | `r"#(\d+)"` matches literal # + digits | | extract_plan_number() | shared.py | 26-41 | Colon-split parsing for `"NUMBER:HASH"` format |
